title: SOLID, Code Smells, and Evidence-Driven Refactoring · Code Recall
summary: Evolve a growing circulation system without changing its verified external behavior. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a refactored maintainable service after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign responsibilities by cohesive reason to change rather than forcing one method or one field per class.
Create extension points around observed stable variation without prebuilding speculative plugin systems.
Expose small client-specific roles so consumers do not depend on operations they cannot or should not use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from typing import Any

Artifact under review: a complete first version of a refactored maintainable service while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The Oop Safe Refactoring record says a complete first version of a refactored maintainable service while every earlier competency remains active looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Oop Safe Refactoring without its stated preconditions, omit the a complete first version of a refactored maintainable service while every earlier competency remains active trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Oop Safe Refactoring for a complete first version of a refactored maintainable service while every earlier competency remains active.
The record names the assumption, applies Oop Safe Refactoring, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
